diagrams.title: C Standard Library Header Files Organization
diagrams.description: Shows the main header files and their associated built-in functions organized by category
elements: stdio.h - printf, scanf, gets, puts, getchar, putchar
stdlib.h - malloc, calloc, free, rand, srand, abs, exit, atoi
string.h - strlen, strcpy, strcat, strcmp, strupr, strlwr
ctype.h - isalpha, isdigit, isalnum, toupper, tolower
math.h - sqrt, pow, sin, cos, tan, floor, ceil, fabs
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for remembering which functions belong to which header files and their categorization.

diagrams.title: String Functions Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how string functions manipulate strings in memory
elements: strcpy: copies complete string to new location
strcat: appends source to end of destination
strlen: counts characters excluding null terminator
Output: copied/appended/compared result with null terminator \0
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the difference between copying, concatenating, comparing, and measuring strings.

diagrams.title: Memory Allocation Functions Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Step-by-step process of dynamic memory allocation and deallocation in C
elements: Step 1: malloc/calloc requests memory from heap
Step 2: System allocates memory or returns NULL if failed
Step 3: Use pointer to access allocated memory
Step 4: free() returns memory to heap
Step 5: Memory becomes available for reuse
Warning: Always check for NULL after allocation, always free memory
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the complete lifecycle of dynamic memory management and preventing memory leaks.
